name: litmus-mcp
description: Connect Claude to Litmus Edge industrial devices through your Litmus MCP Server.
long_description: Gives Claude access to your Litmus Edge deployment: browse DeviceHub devices and tags, read real-time and historical process data, manage Digital Twins, inspect system health, and reach the full Litmus SDK surface. This extension bridges Claude Desktop to a Litmus MCP Server you run on your own network (see https://github.com/litmusautomation/litmus-mcp-server); your credentials are stored in the operating system keychain and sent only to that server.

server_url.title: Litmus MCP Server URL
server_url.description: Base URL of your Litmus MCP Server, e.g. https://mcp.example.com or http://localhost:8000

edge_url.title: Litmus Edge URL
edge_url.description: URL of the Litmus Edge device, e.g. https://192.168.1.50

edge_api_client_id.title: Edge OAuth2 Client ID
edge_api_client_id.description: OAuth2 client ID from Litmus Edge (System > API Access)

edge_api_client_secret.title: Edge OAuth2 Client Secret
edge_api_client_secret.description: OAuth2 client secret from Litmus Edge

nats_source.title: NATS Host (optional)
nats_source.description: Litmus Edge IP for real-time data tools; leave empty to skip

nats_port.title: NATS Port (optional)
nats_port.description: Defaults to 4222 when NATS Host is set

nats_password.title: NATS Password (optional)
nats_password.description: Access token from Litmus Edge (username is not needed)

influx_host.title: InfluxDB Host (optional)
influx_host.description: Litmus Edge IP for historical data tools; leave empty to skip

influx_port.title: InfluxDB Port (optional)
influx_port.description: Defaults to 8086 when InfluxDB Host is set

validate_certificate.title: Validate TLS Certificates
validate_certificate.description: Verify the Litmus Edge HTTPS certificate. Leave off for edges with self-signed certificates (the default).
